# Compute the intersection (shared elements) of two array columns

**Dataset**: `guardian_xql_lab` (composite/array lookup lab)

Two-array overlap has NO single-call form, and the obvious nested-`@element` attempt `arrayfilter(a, array_any(b, "@element" = "@element"))` is a SILENT BUG — the inner `@element` shadows the outer, so the predicate is a tautology and it returns ALL of `a`. The correct idiom: explode one array so its elements become SCALARS, then test each against the other array — `arrayexpand a | filter array_any(b, "@element" = a) | comp values(a) as shared by <key>`. `values()` re-collects the matched elements into the intersection array.

```sql
dataset = guardian_xql_lab
| alter roles = json_extract_scalar_array(roles_json, "$"), peers = json_extract_scalar_array(peer_roles_json, "$")
| arrayexpand roles
| filter array_any(peers, "@element" = roles)
| comp values(roles) as shared by username
| alter shared_csv = arraystring(shared, ","), n = array_length(shared)
| sort desc n
| fields username, shared_csv, n
```

_Live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant; measured cost ~0.0003 CU against a 10-row lookup (data scanned, not query complexity, drives CU)._
